title: Aegiphila quinduensis
taxonomy: Aegiphila quinduensis is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This family, also known as the mint family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Aegiphila quinduensis is a species of plant in the genus Aegiphila, which is native to South America. The classification of Aegiphila quinduensis is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, and then the class Magnoliopsida. intro: Aegiphila quinduensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the Quindío leafy mint. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Aegiphila quinduensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aegiphila quinduensis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues, as well as to support digestive health.
